pattern: large purchase from new device in foreign country
description: Cardholder's details were skimmed and cloned. Fraudster made a large electronics purchase from a device never seen before, in a country the victim had never transacted from. Amount was 6x the victim's average spend.

pattern: rapid small purchases followed by large purchase
description: Cloned card tested with several small transactions under $5 within minutes, then a single large purchase at an electronics retailer. Classic card testing followed by cash-out.

pattern: password reset then immediate high-value transfer
description: Attacker gained access via credential stuffing, changed the password, added a new payee and transferred nearly the full balance within 20 minutes, at 3 AM local time.

pattern: login from new device and new geography
description: Account accessed from a device and IP geolocation never associated with the user. Attacker changed notification settings before initiating transfers to mule accounts.

pattern: many small incoming transfers then single large outgoing
description: Account received dozens of small transfers from unrelated senders over two days, then forwarded the aggregate sum to an overseas account. Typical mule layering behaviour.

pattern: dormant account suddenly routing funds
description: A dormant account with no activity for 8 months suddenly began receiving and immediately forwarding transfers. Device used was shared with three other flagged accounts.

pattern: new account fast ramp-up to credit limit
description: Account opened with a synthetic identity (real SSN fragment, fake name). Small normal purchases for two weeks to build trust, then maxed out available credit on gift cards.

pattern: multiple accounts sharing one device
description: Five newly created accounts with different identities all operated from the same physical device. Each account transacted just below reporting thresholds.

pattern: victim-initiated transfer to fraudster account
description: Victim was socially engineered by a fake bank support call and personally authorised a transfer to a 'safe account' controlled by the fraudster. Transfer was 10x the victim's usual amount.

pattern: OTP interception and instant drain
description: Fraudster phished the victim's one-time passcode and drained the account via three consecutive maximum-value transfers within five minutes.

pattern: burst of tiny online transactions
description: Stolen card numbers validated through a burst of 15 sub-dollar transactions at an online donation page within two minutes, from a single device cycling through many cards.

pattern: purchase, refund to different instrument
description: Fraudster purchased high-value goods and manipulated support into refunding onto a different card. Repeated across several accounts sharing the same device fingerprint.

pattern: large transfer to crypto exchange at odd hours
description: Compromised account transferred a large sum to a crypto exchange at 2 AM, far exceeding the user's typical spend, immediately after a login from a new device.

pattern: structured transfers below limits to exchange
description: Series of transfers each just under the daily limit sent to a crypto exchange over consecutive nights, emptying the account while evading per-transaction alerts.

pattern: bulk gift card purchases after account access
description: Attacker converted stolen balance into retailer gift cards — several maximum-denomination purchases in a row, a pattern almost never seen in legitimate use.

pattern: many transactions in short window
description: Account executed 12 transactions in under ten minutes across different merchants, inconsistent with any prior behaviour. Indicates scripted/automated cash-out.

pattern: cluster of accounts transacting with each other
description: A cluster of six accounts, several already flagged, transacted almost exclusively with each other and shared two devices. Funds cycled to obscure origin before off-ramp.

pattern: new account funnelling to flagged receivers
description: Newly opened account began sending funds exclusively to accounts previously flagged for fraud. Shared device fingerprint with two of the receivers.

pattern: customer disputes own legitimate purchases
description: Customer made purchases then filed chargebacks claiming non-receipt. Pattern repeated across billing cycles; delivery confirmations contradicted every claim.

pattern: seller uses stolen cards to fulfil orders
description: Marketplace seller took orders and fulfilled them using stolen card numbers, pocketing the buyer payments. Detected via mismatched shipping and card billing identities.

pattern: fake employer overpayment and refund request
description: Victim received a fraudulent 'salary overpayment' and was pressured to refund the difference to a third account before the original credit bounced.

pattern: escalating transfers to single overseas beneficiary
description: Victim sent escalating amounts to a single overseas beneficiary over weeks, each transfer larger than the last, depleting savings. No prior relationship to the receiver.

pattern: supplier bank details changed before payment
description: Business email compromise: attacker altered a supplier's bank details on a pending invoice. Payment went to an account with no transaction history.

pattern: card present withdrawals in two distant cities
description: Physical card used for ATM withdrawals in two cities 800km apart within one hour — impossible travel indicating a cloned card in parallel use.

pattern: trial abuse across linked accounts
description: Dozens of trial subscriptions opened from linked accounts sharing payment instruments and devices, each cancelled just before billing.

pattern: employee-linked account receiving waived fees
description: Account linked to an employee device received systematic fee reversals and manual credit adjustments outside policy.

pattern: structured deposits under reporting threshold
description: Cash deposits structured at just under the reporting threshold across multiple branches on the same day, then consolidated and wired abroad.

pattern: high-value purchase from flagged receiver network
description: Purchase routed to a merchant account connected to a known fraud ring; buyer account had prior chargebacks and shared a device with a flagged user.

pattern: logins from two continents within an hour
description: Sessions initiated from IPs on two continents within 40 minutes, followed by a transfer 8x the account average to a first-time receiver.

pattern: long-dormant account making max transfers
description: Account inactive for a year suddenly made three maximum-limit transfers at 4 AM to receivers with no prior relationship, from a newly enrolled device.
